findings.evidence: Lines 46-47 define `cubeVertex x T`, but lines 90-92 state membership only via `∃ z ∈ (S.image fun v => Pi.single v (1 : ℤ)).subsetSum, x + z = y`; the only subset-indexed public theorem at lines 108-111 proves just one direction.
findings.fix: Define `cubeVertices x S` as `S.powerset.image (cubeVertex x)` or add the public membership theorem `y ∈ cubeVertices x S ↔ ∃ T, T ⊆ S ∧ cubeVertex x T = y`, keeping the subset-sum formulation private if useful for proofs.
findings.issue: `mem_cubeVertices` characterizes cube vertices by membership in `(S.image fun v => Pi.single v 1).subsetSum`, even though the cube data and preceding `cubeVertex` API are indexed by subsets `T ⊆ S`; this exposes an overly general additive-subset-sum abstraction to users of plumbing cubes.

summary: The cube-weight definition is at the right plumbing level, but the public vertex-set API is exposed at the subset-sum implementation level rather than the natural subset-of-directions level.
